merger_description:
Oakley Eloa Luxco S.à r.l., a special purpose vehicle incorporated under the laws of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, proposes to acquire all shares in Bond Lux Co-Invest S.à r.l., a private limited company incorporated under the laws of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, which trades as XTEL (the **Acquisition**).

Oakley Eloa Luxco S.à r.l is indirectly controlled by one or more affiliates of Oakley Capital Manager S.a r.l, (**Oakley Manager**) a Luxembourg governed entity advised by Oakley Capital Limited (**OCL**) as investment advisor to Oakley Capital VI Aggregator SCSp and Oakley Capital VI Co-Investment (A) SCSp (together, **Oakley Fund VI**) (together, **Oakley Capital**).

XTEL is headquartered in Leuven, Belgium, and is a global enterprise software provider delivering a cloud native, AI-first revenue management platform for the consumer-packaged goods sector. Its solutions include trade promotion management, trade promotion optimisation, revenue growth management, AI data management, assortment, and retail execution.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Oakley Eloa Luxco S.à r.l., a special purpose vehicle indirectly
controlled by one or more affiliates of Oakley Capital Manager S.a r.l,
(Oakley Manager) a Luxembourg governed entity advised by Oakley
Capital Limited (OCL) as investment advisor to Oakley Capital VI
Aggregator SCSp and Oakley Capital VI Co-Investment (A) SCSp
(together, Oakley Fund VI) (together, Oakley Capital), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the share capital in Bond Lux Co-Invest S.à r.l (XTEL), as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Oakley Capital is a European growth-focused investment firm which
focuses on investing across the core sectors of digital consumer,
technology, business services and education.
XTEL is headquartered in Leuven, Belgium, and is a global enterprise
software provider delivering a cloud-native, AI-first revenue
management platform for the consumer-packaged goods sector. Its
solutions include trade promotion management (TPM), trade
promotion optimisation (TPO), revenue growth management (RGM),
AI data management, assortment, and retail execution.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal or
vertical overlap between Oakley Capital (and its connected entities)
and XTEL in the supply of software solutions for the consumer
packaged goods sector in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
